column1: Lots to decipher. Hope to see you tonight on ABC Action News - WFTS - Tampa Bay and on DPL at 7:30. (No, I don't know if your flight is cancelled yet) 🤣

If I'm in a Zone A area, would it be prudent to reschedule my young child's major surgery with a hefty recovery that is scheduled for tomorrow? It freaks me out to think of having to do the recovery process with no electricity and the special diet. (Yes, I know I'm being paranoid... 😅)
thank you - you're the best!
stop bothering people

I would from personal experience. Make sure you have all the meds ahead of time plenty of snacks and gfood for the special diet due to the surgery and a generator in case of lose of power bc that would. Ache most uncomfortable due to just having surgery. Lots of water and ice on hand maybe an extra cooler with ice to make ice packs etc. we went through this with two teens during a storm a few years back they both got their wisdom teeth taken out and it was a struggle but as long as we had everything we needed we were fine. We didn’t lose electricity for 2 to 3 days, but that was the hard part so we recommend the generator or something to get power during thestorm in case you lose it. For entertainment reasons because no matter what surgery you’re gonna need to be entertained because of the pain. Hope this all helps.
Do we know when to expect it where? I have a trip upcoming. With cones, they usually had at least dates.
Thanks!
Hope it travels fast and gets away from the gulf and the gulf states.
How bad will it hit Crystal River?

Hi Denis, a friend of mine invited me to dinner tomorrow night at 6pm. He lives off Elam Rd in Wesley chapel. Will the storm be developing and threaten our plans? Should I cancel or use the storm as an excuse to arrive empty handed?
I need to know if I need to cancel my rocket launch in October of 2049 based on the latest model run!
just watch for the heat lightning
no… I just looked . Going to be bright and sunny everyday! You good! 🤣😂🤣
winner
Looking forward to seeing you with your hoodie and Diet Dr. Pepper! ♥️

Same as most of them: We know the potential is brewing out there, it's just Way Too early to get a more defined path. Best thing to do is stop panicking, make sure you're stocked up on necessary supplies (water, non-perishables, toiletries, batteries, a weather radio if you have one, Pet food, etc.) & make sure you have a full gas tank. Then just sit back and wait and see.
These storms can be so unpredictable, and even at the last minute when you're almost Certain it's going to make landfall at a particular point, it can shift drastically and come in at a totally different spot. Just don't get caught unprepared. 🤷‍♀️
Noone knows for sure what is going to be, or where is going to head yet. Noone can make decisions on your vacation plans/travel but You. It's like a broken record EVERY storm with these questions.
Yes, Nailed it!
but what about the heat lightning?
Ahh, yeah, I missed touching on that one. Thanks for the assist! 😅
So that's why gas prices jumped today! 🤦🏼‍♀️
Denis, thanks for you being you. I tune in and listen. Don’t need a zillion answers to what no one knows! Thanks for keeping us all aware and calm.
Calm? Half the people are panicking and freaking out already. Because it;s a blob on a map.
I think Denis has the best perspective.
Wondering if I should bring my pet rock inside today or wait until the weekend…. Would hate to see her get blown away or get rained on too much, she doesn’t know how to swim yet!
Wait a minute 🤔 what happened to the model that had this thing going into Louisiana and Alabama? COME BACK! 😆

trust Denis!!

When I lived in Broward , WSVN (channel 7) would be doing live shots outside of home depot the minute a cloud 🌥 would come off the coast of Africa. I really appreciate rule # 7!

So here is what I do not understand. Hurricane season comes every year. Obviously produce and milk cannot be stockpiled but doesn’t everyone buy a little extra TP and water at the start of the season?

It’s about flooding. I’ve lived in Sarasota for most of my life. I have 20 lanterns, two battery radios, plenty of food and water but…these past torrential rains are flooding our house and we live in a no flood zone a billion miles from the Gulf. It’s different now.

most of us do but there are some who like to live on the edge and wait until the last minute for everything.
As for me I’m at 1/2 tank of gas. I’ll fill it tomorrow and then pray the expected heavy rain doesn’t knock out my power.
crap. I think I need gas for my car. 🤦🏼‍♀️Did not think of that!
don't forget some cash for when the electricity goes out and wash all the laundry in the house
Not usually. Always that last minute rush!
hard to stock up on anything when a lot of people can barely afford day by day things when prices went up.
I started last fall.Even buying 1 or 2 things at a time will help over a longer period of time.Easier on the budget too.
Yes I do that. I’m just saying for a lot of people, that’s not a reality.
I have 9 cases of water in season 2-3 outside of season and 3/4 of gas tank at least. Cash you can get when it becomes official.
absolutely! We buy water when it’s on sale and have 9-12 cases on hand at all times. Extra TP when it’s on sale and then non-perishables in hand for about 5 days and hope we never need it.
thinking ahead is not the American way.
they should, but they don't. I had a lady tell me once that she didn't have ANY room for any extra supplies.
Im suppose to fly back to orlando on Monday. AM I good?
Is it really that big of a deal for Tampa? It really wouldn’t be that organized if it comes here

I agree but I also watched Andrew when I lived in Ft Laud., and then Charley and Ian all make shifts within a few hours of landfall that rocked the world of many people. I’m cautious until it passes my area and know it’s not coming back.
Denis keeps saying a ragged system though in the Tampa area. I’m more concerned if it goes to the North Gulf coast. Always be ready though! 👍
I think the worst would be IF it stalled near Tampa, causing a lot of flooding.
Yeah if you live near the coast that’s an annoyance
i do. 1st floor across the street from the gulf and surrounded by canals.... ugh......

ERRR… water temps aren’t everything. You can have a tropical system in 90F water and if the atmospheric conditions don’t allow intensification, then it can’t intensify. Denis never said water temp is the only factor. He knows better, and knows it’s about more than the water temps
